finding: §10.9 (spec line 134) states tp audit --merge 'operates on the result files of a single round, which share one id scheme', making merge intra-round; but acceptance §15.12 (line 179) asserts that tp audit --merge 'deduplicates two rounds' rows for that subject on (role, item_id)'. Intra-round merge (consistent with §10.3's per-role framing and CLAUDE.md's 'per-role audit-result files') can never dedup rows from two separate rounds, so §15.12 is unimplementable as written: an implementer must either make merge span rounds (violating §10.9's single-round guarantee, which is what blocks a positional-vs-slug id comparison inside a merge) or skip the test. The round-5 single-round clarification sharpened §10.9 into direct conflict with this pre-existing test.
suggestion: Cross-round id stability is observable through the prior-round section (§10.2/§10.9), not through merge. Reword §15.12 to verify that a regenerated checklist keeps a subject's id and that a round-2 audit prompt's prior-round section references it by the same item id, and drop the 'two rounds' rows' merge claim.

finding: §11.2 routes a tp done --covered-by close to excluded_from_accuracy ('reported in the existing excluded_from_accuracy count'), but §11.1 states 'tp done performs an implicit claim when a task is still open' and §11.2 classifies tp done's implicit claim as duration_source=implicit with precedence over excluded_from_accuracy ('an implicit-duration task is counted only in implicit_duration even when its near-zero duration would round to 0.0'). An open task closed via tp done --covered-by is therefore duration_source=implicit by §11.1/§11.2, so by the precedence rule it lands in implicit_duration — contradicting the explicit excluded_from_accuracy routing. The spec never states that --covered-by skips the implicit claim or carries no duration_source.
suggestion: State explicitly that tp done --covered-by performs no claim and sets no duration_source (no implementing work, no measured duration), placing it outside the implicit path so it is reported only in excluded_from_accuracy; or add covered-by to the list of close paths that do not implicitly claim in §11.1.

finding: The round-6 addition creates an internal tension within §10.9. The opening states unconditionally 'Every round tp records under v0.30.0+ carries an id_scheme marker — value "slug" — stating which id scheme its rows use', but the new text says 'the marker is recorded on audit rounds and may be recorded on review rounds as a no-op format tag'. 'May be recorded' reads as optional for review rounds, contradicting the mandatory 'every round carries it'; and 'stating which id scheme its rows use' remains semantically false for review rounds (which dedup on (location,class) and use no item ids) even after the 'no-op format tag' recharacterization. Two implementers could write different code (always-write vs audit-only-write). Practical impact is nil — only audit rounds consume the marker — so this is low severity, but the two sentences should be reconciled (e.g. scope the opening to 'every audit round' or change 'may be recorded' to 'is also recorded').

§10.9 permits recording the id_scheme marker on review rounds as 'a no-op format tag' that 'only audit rounds consume'. Review rounds dedup on (location, class) and never use item ids, so the marker feeds no decision there — a stored field with no reader is surface not earned. Recommend dropping the 'may be recorded on review rounds' clause so id_scheme is written on audit rounds only.
